User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Rentipedia application, who can be either a landlord or a renter. This entity stores core user information and contact details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for communication.

whatsappNumber.description: The user's WhatsApp contact number for direct communication.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

role.description: The role of the user within the application, typically 'Landlord' or 'Renter'.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was last updated.

Amenity.title: Amenity
Amenity.description: Represents a distinct amenity that can be associated with a room listing, such as WiFi or AC.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Amenity entity.

name.description: The common name of the amenity (e.g., 'WiFi', 'AC', 'Inverter', 'Water Supply').

description.description: A brief description or clarification of the amenity.

RoomListing.title: RoomListing
RoomListing.description: Represents a room available for rent, including all its details, location, amenities, and pricing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RoomListing entity.

landlordId.description: Reference to the User who posted this room listing. (Relationship: User 1:N RoomListing)

title.description: A concise title for the room listing.

description.description: A detailed description of the room, including its features, conditions, and any special notes.

locationDescription.description: A general description of the room's precise location and neighborhood highlights.

fullAddress.description: The full street address of the room, including house number, street, etc.

city.description: The city where the room is located.

state.description: The state or region where the room is located.

zipCode.description: The postal code of the room's location.

nearestCommunicationOptions.description: A list of nearby public transportation or communication options (e.g., 'Bus Stop', 'Railway Station', 'Metro Station').

monthlyRent.description: The monthly rent amount for the room.

securityDeposit.description: The security deposit required for renting the room, if any.

availableFrom.description: The date from which the room is available for rent.

amenityIds.description: References to Amenities available in the room. (Relationship: Amenity N:N RoomListing)

isAvailable.description: Indicates if the room is currently available for rent.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the room listing was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the room listing was last updated.

Photo.title: Photo
Photo.description: Represents a single photo associated with a room listing, including its URL and display order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Photo entity.

roomListingId.description: Reference to the RoomListing this photo belongs to. (Relationship: RoomListing 1:N Photo)

url.description: The URL where the photo image can be accessed.

caption.description: A short descriptive caption for the photo.

order.description: The numerical order for displaying the photo within its associated listing.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the photo was uploaded.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the photo record was last updated.

SocialPost.title: SocialPost
SocialPost.description: Represents an AI-generated social media post related to room listings or search requests, ready for sharing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SocialPost entity.

authorUserId.description: Reference to the User who created or initiated this social post. (Relationship: User 1:N SocialPost)

roomListingId.description: Optional reference to the RoomListing this social post is about. (Relationship: RoomListing 1:N SocialPost)

targetPlatform.description: The social media platform for which the post is intended (e.g., 'Facebook', 'WhatsApp').

postContent.description: The AI-generated text content of the social media post.

generatedImageUrl.description: Optional URL for an image associated with the post, either generated by AI or linked from the listing.

status.description: The current status of the social post (e.g., 'Draft', 'Scheduled', 'Published', 'Failed').

scheduledPublishTime.description: The planned date and time for the post to be published.

actualPublishTime.description: The actual date and time when the post was published.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the social post record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the social post record was last updated.

UserChannel.title: UserChannel
UserChannel.description: Represents a social media channel (e.g., Facebook group, WhatsApp group) configured by a user for automated sharing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserChannel entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this configured channel. (Relationship: User 1:N UserChannel)

platformType.description: The type of social media platform (e.g., 'Facebook', 'WhatsApp').

channelName.description: The user-friendly name or identifier of the specific channel (e.g., 'My Facebook Group for Renters').

externalChannelId.description: An optional external ID provided by the social media platform for the channel (e.g., Facebook Group ID).

isConfigured.description: Indicates if the channel is properly configured and ready for automated sharing.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user channel was configured.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user channel configuration was last updated.

Inquiry.title: Inquiry
Inquiry.description: Represents a direct communication or inquiry from a renter to a landlord about a specific room listing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Inquiry entity.

renterUserId.description: Reference to the User who sent the inquiry (the renter). (Relationship: User 1:N Inquiry)

landlordUserId.description: Reference to the User who is the landlord of the listing being inquired about. (Relationship: User 1:N Inquiry)

roomListingId.description: Reference to the RoomListing related to this inquiry. (Relationship: RoomListing 1:N Inquiry)

messageContent.description: The textual content of the inquiry message.

status.description: The current status of the inquiry (e.g., 'New', 'Read', 'Replied', 'Closed').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the inquiry was sent.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the inquiry record was last updated.

LegalForm.title: LegalForm
LegalForm.description: Represents a generated online legal form or agreement related to a room rental deal, ensuring trust and compliance.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LegalForm entity.

roomListingId.description: Reference to the RoomListing for which this legal form was created. (Relationship: RoomListing 1:N LegalForm)

renterUserId.description: Reference to the User who is the renter in this legal agreement. (Relationship: User 1:N LegalForm)

landlordUserId.description: Reference to the User who is the landlord in this legal agreement. (Relationship: User 1:N LegalForm)

documentContentUrl.description: The URL to the generated legal document, typically a PDF, stored externally.

formDetails.description: Optional serialized data of the form fields that were filled out to generate the legal document.

agreementDate.description: The date and time when the legal agreement was established or signed.

status.description: The current status of the legal form (e.g., 'Draft', 'Awaiting Renter Signature', 'Signed by Both', 'Completed').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the legal form record was initiated.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the legal form record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles (landlords and renters). Access is restricted to the user whose ID matches `{userId}`. This collection holds general user information. Denormalization: None for authorization.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching 'request.auth.uid'.

definition.description: A sentinel collection to identify users who are landlords. An empty document at this path signifies the user has the 'Landlord' role for DBAC (Database Access Control). Denormalization: None, relies on existence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who is a landlord, matching 'request.auth.uid'.

definition.description: A sentinel collection to identify users who are renters. An empty document at this path signifies the user has the 'Renter' role for DBAC. Denormalization: None, relies on existence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who is a renter, matching 'request.auth.uid'.

definition.description: Stores global amenities data (e.g., 'WiFi', 'AC'). This collection is publicly readable for all users and typically writable only by administrators. Denormalization: None.

params.name: amenityId
params.description: The unique identifier of the amenity.

definition.description: Stores all room listings available for rent. This collection is publicly readable for search and browsing by all users (QAP compliant). Write access (create, update, delete) is restricted to the 'landlordId' specified within each document. Includes denormalized 'landlordId' for authorization independence.

params.name: roomListingId
params.description: The unique identifier for the room listing.

definition.description: Stores photos associated with a specific room listing. These photos are publicly readable as they are part of a public listing (QAP compliant). Write access is restricted to the 'landlordId' of the parent room listing, which is denormalized into each photo document for authorization independence.

params.name: roomListingId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent room listing.

params.name: photoId
params.description: The unique identifier for the photo.

definition.description: Stores AI-generated social media posts created by a specific user. Access is restricted to the user whose ID matches `{userId}` in the path (path-based ownership). Includes denormalized 'authorUserId' for internal consistency and verification.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who authored the social post.

params.name: socialPostId
params.description: The unique identifier for the social media post.

definition.description: Stores social media channels configured by a specific user for automated sharing. Access is restricted to the user whose ID matches `{userId}` in the path (path-based ownership). Includes denormalized 'userId' for internal consistency and verification.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who configured the channel.

params.name: channelId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user's configured channel.

definition.description: Stores direct inquiries between renters and landlords about a room listing. This collection allows read/write access to both the 'renterUserId' and 'landlordUserId' specified within the document (collaborative access). Both IDs are denormalized for authorization independence and QAP compliance (requires explicit query filtering by `renterUserId` or `landlordUserId`).

params.name: inquiryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the inquiry.

definition.description: Stores online legal forms and agreements related to rental deals. This collection allows read/write access to both the 'renterUserId' and 'landlordUserId' specified within the document (collaborative access). Both IDs are denormalized for authorization independence and QAP compliance (requires explicit query filtering by `renterUserId` or `landlordUserId`).

params.name: legalFormId
params.description: The unique identifier for the legal form.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for Rentipedia prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization by strictly adhering to the core design principles. The primary strategy employed is **Authorization Independence via Denormalization**, which eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules to retrieve parent or related document data for authorization decisions. This is crucial for maintaining atomic operations and simplifying rule debugging. For instance, `RoomListing` documents contain `landlordId`, and `Photo` documents (within a subcollection of `RoomListing`) also denormalize `landlordId` to ensure the owner can manage them without fetching the parent listing. Similarly, `Inquiry` and `LegalForm` documents denormalize both `renterUserId` and `landlordUserId` to grant explicit access to all involved parties.

**Structural Segregation** is applied by ensuring that all documents within a collection share the same security posture. For example, `RoomListing` documents are all publicly readable, enabling robust Query-as-Permission (QAP) models. Private user-specific data, such as `SocialPost` and `UserChannel`, are housed within `/users/{userId}/...` subcollections, clearly delineating their private nature.

**Access Modeling** standardizes authorization patterns:
1.  **Private Data:** User profiles, social posts, and configured channels are secured using path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/...`).
2.  **Public Data with Owner Write:** `RoomListing` and its `Photo` subcollections are publicly readable for search functionality, but write access is restricted to the specific `landlordId` denormalized within each document.
3.  **Collaborative Data:** `Inquiry` and `LegalForm` entities are stored in top-level collections, with `renterUserId` and `landlordUserId` denormalized within each document. This allows both parties to access the document and supports QAPs by requiring client-side queries to filter by the `request.auth.uid` on either of these fields (`where('renterUserId', '==', request.auth.uid)` or `where('landlordUserId', '==', request.auth.uid)`).
4.  **DBAC (Database Access Control):** User roles ('Landlord', 'Renter') are handled using dedicated sentinel collections (`/landlords/{userId}`, `/renters/{userId}`). This 'Existence over Content' approach allows security rules to check for a user's role without `get()` calls to the user's profile document, thereby fully supporting Authorization Independence. For example, a rule for creating a `RoomListing` can simply check `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/landlords/$(request.auth.uid))`.

This design effectively supports **QAPs** for all collections. Collections like `room_listings` and `room_listings/{roomListingId}/photos` are fully public for reads, so any `list` query is implicitly permitted. For multi-party access collections like `inquiries` and `legal_forms`, the rules will explicitly enforce that any `list` operation *must* include a filter that constrains results to the requesting user's `renterUserId` or `landlordUserId` field, preventing unauthorized broad fetches.

**Data Clarity and Predictability** are ensured through consistent naming conventions (e.g., `ownerId`, descriptive wildcards like `{roomListingId}`) and explicit state modeling (`status` fields for `SocialPost`, `Inquiry`, `LegalForm`, and `isAvailable` for `RoomListing`).
